entries.definition:
q–ḍ–ḍ to swoop down, to descend, to charge; to collapse, to
tumble; pebble, to be pebbly; to pierce, to bore; to become dusty;
to be rough. Of this root, يَنْقَضّ yanqaḍḍ occurs once in the Qur’an.

يَنْقَضّ yanqaḍḍ [imperf. of v. VII, intrans.] to crumble, to
collapse, to crack up, to fall down, to tumble down (18:77) فَوَجَدَا
فِيهَا جِدَارًا يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَنْقَضَّ فَأَقَامَهُ they found a wall in it that was on the
point of collapsing and he supported it.
